title: Scroll, Scope, Aberration, Invert & Radial
description: Sticky scroll choreography, cross-DOM timeline-scope, chromatic aberration text, inverting cursor, and CSS trig radial menu.

drops.title: Sticky Grid Scroll
drops.description: 12 gradient tiles pinned in the viewport animate in column-by-column from opposite directions as you scroll — pure CSS.
drops.concept: Uses position:sticky to pin a scene while its container is 550vh tall. CSS animation-timeline: scroll(root) drives each column's tile-in keyframe, with staggered animation-range values (0-20%, 9-29%, etc.) so columns arrive in sequence as scroll progress increases. The grid then scales up via a second animation. Zero JavaScript required.

drops.title: CSS timeline-scope
drops.description: A sticky sidebar panel changes as you scroll a text column in a separate DOM branch — linked by CSS timeline-scope, no JS.
drops.concept: Each text section declares a view-timeline-name (--t1 through --t5). The common .wrapper ancestor sets timeline-scope: --t1, ..., --t5, promoting those timelines so their sibling (the sticky panel's cards) can reference them via animation-timeline. Each panel card uses animation-range: entry 0% exit 100% to fade in/out in sync with its section's viewport visibility.
drops.source: CSS-Tricks timeline-scope almanac + MDN Scroll-Driven Animations

drops.title: Chromatic Aberration Text
drops.description: Hover large headlines to split them into red and cyan channels that shift apart — mix-blend-mode: screen makes it additive.
drops.concept: Each .glitch element carries ::before (red) and ::after (cyan) pseudo-elements with content: attr(data-text). On hover, clip-path reveals them and a CSS keyframe animation translates them in opposite X directions. mix-blend-mode: screen on both pseudo-elements creates the additive RGB-split look. A strength slider updates a CSS custom property to tune the offset live.
drops.source: Multiple CodePen authors — classic technique resurging in 2026

drops.title: Inverting Cursor Circle
drops.description: A spring-lagged white circle follows the cursor and inverts every color beneath it via mix-blend-mode: difference — no canvas.
drops.concept: A fixed white 48px circle uses mix-blend-mode: difference, which subtracts its color (white = 255,255,255) from every pixel beneath it — producing the exact color complement. JavaScript applies exponential spring easing (cx += (tx-cx)*0.14 per frame) so the circle lags naturally. It expands over hover targets and shrinks over links.
drops.source: dev.to/gdlazcano + codepen.io/clementGir — popular pattern going viral in 2026

drops.title: CSS Radial Menu
drops.description: Three radial menus with 4, 6, and 8 items positioned in a circle using CSS calc(cos()) and calc(sin()) — no JS geometry.
drops.concept: Each menu item uses CSS custom properties --i (index) and --n (count) to compute its angle (360deg/n*i), then positions itself at left: calc(50% + cos(--angle) * --radius - half-size) and top: calc(50% + sin(--angle) * --radius - half-size). Opening the menu adds an .open class which sets opacity:1 and scale:1 with staggered transition-delay. CSS trig functions replace all JS positioning math.
drops.source: Una Kravets una.im/radial-menu — CSS trig + Popover API
